entries.title: Commentaries on the Twelve Davidic Psalms on psalms 37:38
entries.excerpt: But the unjust will perish together; the remnants of the wicked will be destroyed. The remnants of the righteous are virtues; the remnants of the wicked are wickedness and the sin of treachery. They will be wiped away, so that they will not exist.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE PSALMS 37 on psalms 37:38
entries.excerpt: The wicked will perish when salvation comes to the just from God, their protector in time of tribulation. The Lord helps and frees them and snatches them from sinners and saves them since they hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.

entries.title: ON THE PSALMS - Psalm 37 on psalms 37:38
entries.excerpt:
"But the transgressors shall be destroyed in the self-same thing" (ver. 38). What is meant by, "in the self-same thing"? It means for ever: or all together in one and the same destruction.

"The remainder of the wicked shall be cut off." Now there is "(a remainder) for the man that maketh peace:" they therefore who are not peace-makers, are ungodly. For, "Blessed are the peace-makers: for they shall be called the children of God."

entries.title: Exposition on the Psalms of David on psalms 37:38
entries.excerpt: Next he sets forth the condemnation of the wicked. The wicked have two things. First, they themselves perish; second, they leave nothing behind them. Therefore he says, "But the unjust will perish." Job 4: "Because no one understands, they will perish forever." "And together the remnants of the wicked will be destroyed." 1 Cor. 2: "Which will perish with them in Gehenna." Or "remnants," that is, all that they had -- namely, riches that perish, fame that rots, offspring that will perish. Job 22: "Has not their prosperity been cut off, and will not fire devour their remnants?"
